title: atriplex oblongifolia var. patula
intro: Atriplex oblongifolia var. patula, also known as Pat's orach, is a type of shrubby plant that belongs to the amaranth family. The leaves and stems of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pat's orach include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Pat's orach can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed for its medicinal and health benefits.
